component.name: Checkbox
component.description: An input control that lets a user select one or more options from a set, toggle a single setting on/off, or — via the indeterminate state — represent a partially-selected group.
component.note: 14px sharp box, forest.500 fill when on, white mark. Hover/press/focus are real CSS pseudo-states (no `state` prop), matching Button. Indeterminate takes priority over checked. Synced to Figma '❖ Checkbox'.

useCases: Select one or more options from a list (multi-select)
Toggle a single boolean setting on/off
Accept terms / opt-in within a form
"Select all" parent row — use isIndeterminate when only some children are selected

commonPatterns.name: Checkbox group
commonPatterns.description: A vertical stack of related checkboxes under a group label. Each is independently selectable.

commonPatterns.name: Select-all parent
commonPatterns.description: A parent checkbox is checked when all children are, indeterminate when only some are, unchecked when none are.

commonPatterns.name: Required consent
commonPatterns.description: A single required checkbox gating form submission, with a danger asterisk.
commonPatterns.jsx: <Checkbox label='I agree to the terms' isRequired isInvalid={submitted && !agreed} />

commonPatterns.name: Controlled toggle
commonPatterns.description: Controlled via isChecked + onChange to drive a single setting.

antiPatterns.scenario: Using a checkbox for a single, instantly-applied on/off setting
antiPatterns.reason: An immediate-effect binary setting reads better as a switch; checkboxes imply a value confirmed on submit.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use Toggle for instant settings; keep Checkbox for form values committed on save.

antiPatterns.scenario: Using a checkbox to choose exactly one option from a mutually-exclusive set
antiPatterns.reason: Checkboxes allow multiple selections; single-choice semantics confuse users and assistive tech.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use Radio / Radio group for mutually-exclusive choices.

antiPatterns.scenario: Setting isIndeterminate together with isChecked on a leaf checkbox
antiPatterns.reason: Indeterminate is only meaningful on a parent summarizing children; the dash takes priority and hides the check.
antiPatterns.alternative: Reserve isIndeterminate for select-all parents; use isChecked on leaves.

antiPatterns.reason: The box has no accessible name and is unusable by screen readers.
antiPatterns.alternative: Always pass label, or set aria-label for a standalone checkbox (e.g. a table-row selector).

slots.label: Optional — text beside the box (omit only for standalone checkboxes, then set aria-label)

interactions.hover: unchecked → neutral.50 fill; checked/indeterminate → forest.700 fill
interactions.press: unchecked → neutral.200 fill (danger.100 when invalid); checked/indeterminate → forest.900 fill
interactions.indeterminate: forest.500 fill + white dash; set via the native indeterminate DOM property and prioritized over checked
interactions.invalid: danger.500 border in every check state; fill stays forest when checked; sets aria-invalid
interactions.disabled: neutral.100 fill, neutral.400 mark, cursor not-allowed

behavior.controlled: Pass isChecked + onChange for controlled; omit isChecked and pass defaultChecked for uncontrolled.

accessibility.minTargetHeight: label row is clickable; box is 14px with a surrounding 20px line-height hit area
accessibility.indeterminateState: set on the native input via the indeterminate DOM property (never an attribute); exposed to AT as aria-checked=mixed
accessibility.keyboardSupport: Space toggles; Tab moves focus in/out
accessibility.screenReader: Native input drives the accessible name from the wrapping label; standalone checkboxes must carry aria-label
accessibility.contrastNote: White mark on forest.500 fill and neutral.800 label text both meet AA.

figmaDivergences.figma: explicit state property (default / hover / press / focus)
figmaDivergences.reason: Matches Button; pseudo-states are driven by the browser rather than a prop.

aiHints.context: Reach for Checkbox when the user can select zero, one, or many options, or to capture a boolean form value committed on submit. Use isIndeterminate only on a select-all parent. For mutually-exclusive choices use Radio; for instantly-applied settings use Toggle.
neverUseWhen: Exactly one option must be chosen from a set — use Radio / Radio group
The setting applies instantly with no submit — use Toggle
It is a leaf option and you set isIndeterminate — reserve indeterminate for select-all parents
There is no label and no aria-label — the control has no accessible name
